title: Surgery Appointments - Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP
meta_description: I hold in-person advice surgeries at different locations across the constituency which you can attend in person or via Zoom. You can book an appointment online.
headings: h2: Need to cancel or running late?
text_excerpt: Surgery Appointments - Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP Home News How I can help Newsletter Tours of Parliament Visits and Diary Surgery Appointments I hold in-person advice surgeries at different locations across the constituency. Surgery appointments are pre-booked only – I won’t be able to see you without an appointment. To make an appointment please follow one of the links below. For all sessions you can choose to attend in person or online via Zoom. Behaviour Policy There will be a security presence for your safety and mine. By booking an appointment you will be acknowledging my Acceptable Behaviour Policy . Need to cancel or running late? If you cannot attend your appointment, please let me know as soon as you can by calling my office or sending me an email , or by following the link in the email if you received one. This means I can make the appointment available to other people who need it. Appointments are 15 minutes long and run back-to-back. If you’re late I won’t be able to see you for the full 15 minutes, and I might not be able to see you at all. If you miss your appointment without letting me know, someone from my office will contact you in the next few days to ask if you still need to speak to me and give you the opportunity to book another appointment. If you miss a second appointment without letting me know, I may not accept further appointment bookings from you; if this happens I will explain how else you can ask for my help if you still need it. Book an appointment May Quorn – Quorn Football Club – Saturday 2nd May 2026 – book now Loughborough – The Arc Community Hub – Friday 29th May 2026 – book now June Shepshed – Charnwood Road Community Centre – Saturday 13th June 2026 – book now Dr. Jeevun Sandher MP for Loughborough, Shepshed and the Villages Site content Home News How I can help Newsletter Tours of Parliament Visits and Diary Policies Privacy Policy Acceptable behaviour
